title: Ferulago westii

Ferulago westii is a species of Ferulago that is native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Ferulago westii includes its placement in the order Apiales and the subclass Rosidae. intro: Ferulago westii, also known as West's sweet cicely, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, characterized by its fern-like foliage and white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from digestive issues, inflammation, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ferulago westii include essential oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are primarily responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Ferulago westii is used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in the form of dried flowers and roots, which are often combined with other herbs to create medicinal preparations.
